generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance HeatbedActuator : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Primary actuator for temperature regulation controlled via bang-bang control. Constrained by upper limit (UL) and lower limit (LL) safety protocols."
]

instance FanActuator : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Distributes heat uniformly inside the styrofoam box controlled via heating duration (H) commands for temperature regulation."
]

instance BangBangController : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Implements bang-bang control logic with statechart parameters: LL (lower threshold), UL (upper threshold), H (heating duration), C (waiting duration). Communicates via RabbitMQ server."
]

instance IncubatorControllerLogic : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Central controller logic integrating bang-bang control and statechart parameters for temperature regulation in the styrofoam box."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance InternalTemperatureSensor1 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Internal temperature sensor monitoring the styrofoam box interior for thermal regulation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTempData
]

instance InternalTemperatureSensor2 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Second internal temperature sensor providing redundant measurements of styrofoam box interior temperature."
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTempData
]

instance ExternalTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "External temperature sensor monitoring ambient conditions outside the styrofoam box."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ExternalTempData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance InternalTempData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor1, InternalTemperatureSensor2
]

instance ExternalTempData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ExternalTemperatureSensor
]

instance ActuatorStateData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom BangBangController
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance ModelAThermal : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simplified 2-parameter thermal model for basic temperature approximation within the styrofoam box."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TemperatureDataStream
]

instance ModelBThermal : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Enhanced 4-parameter thermal model capturing heatbed thermal inertia for accurate temperature prediction."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo KalmanFilter, TemperatureDataStream
]

instance TemperatureDataStream : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Continuous temperature data stream from internal/external sensors for real-time monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:fromData InternalTempData, ExternalTempData
]

instance ControllerParametersData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Data containing controller parameters (LL, UL, H, C) for temperature regulation."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ActuatorStateData
]

// C11: Enablers
instance RabbitMQServer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Open-source message broker facilitating real-time asynchronous communication between controller, actuators, and digital twin components."
    DTDFVocab:enables TemperatureDataStream, ControllerParametersData
]

instance RaspberryPiController : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Central embedded controller interfacing with sensors/actuators via RabbitMQ for system coordination."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BangBangController
]

instance TimeSeriesDatabase : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Database storing real-time temperature data for visualization and historical analysis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TemperatureDataStream
]

instance TrackingSimulator : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Automated calibration tool for dynamically updating Model B parameters using real-time sensor data."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelBThermal
]

instance KalmanFilter : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "State estimation algorithm correlating sensor data with Model B for estimating non-measured heatbed temperature."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelBThermal, TemperatureDataStream
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides real-time visualization of temperature trends via dashboard using RabbitMQ-driven data streams."
    DTDFVocab:provides TemperatureTrendInsight
]

instance AnomalyDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Detects temperature deviations from expected behavior using Kalman filter and Model B predictions."
    DTDFVocab:provides AnomalyDetectionInsight
]

instance CalibrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automated calibration of Model B using tracking simulator and real-time data for improved thermal predictions."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelAccuracyImprovementAction
]

instance ControllerOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Performs what-if simulations to optimize controller parameters (LL, UL, H, C) for improved performance."
    DTDFVocab:provides ParameterOptimizationAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance TemperatureTrendInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization of temperature trends (internal/external) for monitoring incubator conditions."
]

instance AnomalyDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detected deviations from normal thermal behavior (e.g., temperature spikes due to external factors)."
]

instance ModelAccuracyImprovementAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated recalibration of Model B using tracking simulator to enhance thermal prediction accuracy."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ParameterOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Co-simulation-based optimization of controller parameters (LL, UL, H, C) for improved temperature regulation."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance SelfAdaptationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated self-adaptation workflow: anomaly detection → data collection → model recalibration → controller reconfiguration."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C1: System Under Study
instance ThermalIncubatorSystem : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Controlled thermal environment system for precise temperature regulation using styrofoam insulation, heatbed, and fan."
]

instance PhysicalTwin : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Physical implementation of the incubator system including styrofoam box, heatbed, fan, and temperature sensors."
    base:contains HeatbedActuator, FanActuator, InternalTemperatureSensor1, InternalTemperatureSensor2, ExternalTemperatureSensor
]

instance DigitalTwin : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Virtual representation of the incubator system including thermal models, Kalman filter, and controller logic."
    base:contains ModelAThermal, ModelBThermal, KalmanFilter, RabbitMQServer, TemperatureDataStream
]

instance IncubatorEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains PhysicalTwin, DigitalTwin
]


instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
    **Command Types & Formats:**
    - Control commands include:
      - **Heating signals** (turning on/off the heatbed).
      - **Fan control commands** (distribution of heat inside the box).
    - Commands are sent via **RabbitMQ** messages to the low-level driver.

    **Control Logic & Parameters:**
    - Controller implements a **bang-bang control strategy** with parameters:
      - **LL (Lower Limit)** – Temperature threshold for heating initiation.
      - **UL (Upper Limit)** – Temperature threshold for heating cessation.
      - **H (Heating Duration)** – Duration the heatbed remains active.
      - **C (Waiting Duration)** – Delay after actuation to prevent overshoot.

    **Feedback Systems & Validation:**
    - Controller receives temperature measurements from sensors (internal/external).
    - **Statechart-based control** (Figure 8) ensures sequential execution of commands.
    - **No explicit validation/authentication** mechanisms mentioned for commands (only implied via RabbitMQ’s message ordering guarantees).
    - **No explicit confirmation** of command execution (e.g., acknowledgments from actuators).

    **Conflict Resolution:**
    - Implicitly handled via **RabbitMQ’s message ordering** (prioritized execution of commands).
    - Safe protocol in the low-level driver prevents unsafe states.
  "
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "
    - **Periodic sensor readings**: Temperature data is read periodically by the low-level driver (no explicit frequency mentioned, but implied to be in the order of seconds/minutes for real-time control).
    - **Controller actuation delays**: The controller implements a **waiting duration (C)** after each heating actuation to prevent overshoot, suggesting a **latency-dependent control loop** (e.g., ~10–60 seconds for stabilization).
    - **Simulation/calibration time scales**: Model calibration and anomaly detection (e.g., Kalman filter) operate in **real-time or near-real-time** (implied by the self-adaptation loop).
    - **Temporal granularity**: Data is processed in discrete time steps tied to physical actuation events (e.g., heating/fan commands) and sensor readings.
  "
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "
    - **Architecture Pattern**:
      - **CPS-Based Digital Twin Framework**: The DT integrates:
        - **Cyber Components**: RabbitMQ server, low-level driver, controller (Raspberry Pi), and software models (Model A/B, Kalman filter).
        - **Physical Components**: The incubator’s hardware (heatbed, fan, sensors).
        - **Calibration Algorithms**: Automated calibration of models (Model B) using real-time sensor data.
        - **Optimization Loop**: Self-adaptation via a **closed-loop process** (anomaly detection → data collection → model recalibration → controller reconfiguration).

    - **Component Integration**:
      - **Message-Broker Orchestration**: RabbitMQ acts as the **central coordination hub**, synchronizing data between:
        - PT (low-level driver → temperature data),
        - DT (Kalman filter, models, visualization dashboard),
        - Controller (bang-bang logic).
      - **Temporal Orchestration**:
        - **Controller-DT Loop**: Controller commands (fan/heatbed) trigger DT actions (e.g., anomaly detection, calibration) within the **waiting/heating cycle** (H + C).
        - **Self-Adaptation Workflow**: Implemented as an 8-step iterative process (anomaly → data collection → calibration → reconfiguration → simulation → controller update).

    - **Resource Management**:
      - **Hardware**: Raspberry Pi handles control logic; RabbitMQ manages message queues.
      - **Software**: Models (Model A/B) and Kalman filter are co-simulated with PT data.
      - **No explicit cloud/edge deployment**: All components are on-premise (Raspberry Pi + RabbitMQ).
  "
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
    **Development and Deployment Phases:**
    - **Modeling Phase:**
      - Creation of abstract representations (e.g., thermal models of the incubator) to understand system behavior.
      - Two simulation models developed:
        - **Model A**: Simplified with 2 free parameters (basic temperature prediction).
        - **Model B**: Enhanced with 4 free parameters (better captures heatbed thermal inertia).
      - Calibration methods applied to refine models (e.g., tracking simulator for on-demand calibration).

    - **Integration Phase:**
      - Combination of models, data, and algorithms into a cohesive Digital Twin framework.
      - Integration with Physical Twin (PT) via sensor data collection (e.g., temperature sensors inside/outside the styrofoam box).
      - Use of RabbitMQ for real-time communication between PT components (low-level driver, controller).

    - **Operational Phase:**
      - Continuous monitoring of the PT via sensors (e.g., periodic temperature readings).
      - Real-time visualization of incubator status via time-series databases and dashboards.
      - Self-adaptation loop for dynamic controller reconfiguration (e.g., anomaly detection triggers recalibration).

    **Lifecycle Governance:**
    - Open-source availability (GitHub repository) for reproducibility and community collaboration.
    - Version-controlled hardware/software specifications (GitHub repository).
    - Open-source technologies (RabbitMQ, Kalman filter) ensure transparency and modularity.
    - Future work focuses on iterative model refinement and self-adaptation capabilities.
  "
]

    - **No explicit mention of cybersecurity protocols** (e.g., encryption, authentication) in the provided documents, but the DT’s reliance on **calibration and monitoring** implies implicit fail-safe mechanisms for maintaining system integrity.
